step_rationale: PMN-released enzyme marking NET formation and immunothrombotic inflammation.
mechanism: Neutrophil elastase is released by activated neutrophils during NETosis and serves as a marker of neutrophil extracellular trap (NET)-driven immunothrombotic inflammation destabilizing the atherosclerotic plaque.

references.title: Biological basis and pathological relevance of microvascular thrombosis.
Coronary neutrophil extracellular trap burden and deoxyribonuclease activity in ST-elevation acute coronary syndrome are predictors of ST-segment resolution and infarct size.
Neutrophils, neutrophil extracellular traps and interleukin-17 associate with the organisation of thrombi in acute myocardial infarction.
